div1.entryFree: Yew, the tree Ta<*>us baccata (planted in churchyards, and therefore emblem of death): my shroud of white, stuck all with y. Tw. II, 4, 56. bows of double-fatal y. R2 III, 2, 117. would bind me here unto the body of a dismal y. Tit. II, 3, 107. slips of y. slivered in the moon's eclipse, Mcb. IV, 1, 27. In Rom. V, 3, 3 and 137 the spurious Q1 and M. Edd. yew-trees, the authentic O. Edd. young trees.
